project.name: office-stencil
project.description: A reusable Office document template render engine for DOCX, XLSX, and PPTX.

nodes.name: api.py
nodes.summary: Optional FastAPI service exposing health, metrics, and Office-template rendering endpoints for trusted internal callers, with bounded template-path resolution and in-process render telemetry.

nodes.name: RenderMetrics
nodes.summary: Thread-safe in-memory counters that record render successes, failures, durations, formats, and failure stages for one API process.

nodes.name: create_app
nodes.summary: Builds the optional FastAPI application, lazily imports API dependencies, and registers health, metrics, and render routes around a configurable renderer.

nodes.name: _template_path_from_payload
nodes.summary: Validates the request template path and, when a template root is configured, prevents resolved paths from escaping that root.

nodes.name: _output_format_from_payload
nodes.summary: Normalizes an explicit output format or derives it from the template suffix, rejecting malformed request values.

nodes.name: _media_type_for
nodes.summary: Maps supported output formats to Office or PDF media types with a binary fallback.

nodes.name: _http_exception
nodes.summary: Lazily constructs a FastAPI HTTPException while preserving the package's optional API dependency boundary.

nodes.name: cli.py
nodes.summary: Typer command-line interface that loads JSON render data, dispatches Office template rendering, and writes the resulting document with user-friendly errors.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Defines the Typer application callback and CLI command-group entry point.

nodes.name: render_command
nodes.summary: Loads and validates a JSON object, invokes the public renderer, and writes the rendered bytes to a requested output path.

nodes.name: docx.py
nodes.summary: DOCX renderer that validates a template path, delegates Jinja-compatible WordprocessingML rendering to docxtpl, and returns the serialized document bytes.

nodes.name: render_docx
nodes.summary: Validates and loads a DOCX template, renders it with docxtpl using mapping data, and serializes the result to bytes with stage-specific errors.

nodes.name: errors.py
nodes.summary: Defines the Stencil exception hierarchy, including unsupported-format errors and stage-aware render failures that retain template context.

nodes.name: StencilError
nodes.summary: Base exception used to distinguish Stencil failures from unrelated runtime errors.

nodes.name: UnsupportedFormatError
nodes.summary: Signals unsupported template suffixes or requested output-format combinations.

nodes.name: RenderError
nodes.summary: Carries render-stage and template-path context and incorporates those diagnostics into the exception message.

nodes.name: pptx.py
nodes.summary: PPTX rendering engine that edits Open Packaging Convention parts directly, expands slide-level Jinja loops, renders text across XML runs, and rebuilds presentation relationships and content types.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses zipfile and ElementTree to manipulate Open Packaging Convention and PresentationML parts without a high-level presentation library.

nodes.name: render_pptx
nodes.summary: Loads a PPTX ZIP package, plans and renders slide content, rewrites presentation metadata, and returns a compressed rendered package.

nodes.name: _read_slide_refs
nodes.summary: Resolves the ordered presentation slide IDs and relationship targets into package-part references.

nodes.name: _build_render_plan
nodes.summary: Builds the final ordered slide plan, cloning loop-body slides once per evaluated item while retaining ordinary slides.

nodes.name: _find_slide_loop_blocks
nodes.summary: Scans slide paragraph text for balanced Jinja for/endfor markers and returns validated slide-loop boundaries.

nodes.name: _slide_paragraph_texts
nodes.summary: Collects paragraph text from DrawingML XML while joining text split across multiple runs.

nodes.name: _render_planned_slides
nodes.summary: Renders each slide in a prepared plan with the context assigned to that slide.

nodes.name: _render_slide_xml
nodes.summary: Renders Jinja markup across DrawingML text runs and rewrites each affected paragraph into valid slide XML.

nodes.name: _rewrite_presentation
nodes.summary: Reconciles slide parts, slide IDs, relationships, and content-type declarations with the rendered slide plan.

nodes.name: _clone_slide_part
nodes.summary: Copies a slide XML part and its optional relationships part to a new package path.

nodes.name: _max_slide_number
nodes.summary: Finds the largest numeric slide-part suffix in the PPTX package.

nodes.name: _is_slide_part
nodes.summary: Tests whether a package member follows the canonical numbered slide-part path.

nodes.name: _next_relationship_id
nodes.summary: Allocates the first unused sequential presentation relationship identifier.

nodes.name: _slide_relationships_path
nodes.summary: Converts a slide-part path to its companion relationships-part path.

nodes.name: _presentation_target_to_part
nodes.summary: Normalizes a presentation relationship target into a package-relative part path.

nodes.name: _part_to_presentation_target
nodes.summary: Converts a package part name into a relationship target relative to the presentation part.

nodes.name: _evaluate_iterable
nodes.summary: Evaluates a Jinja loop expression and requires a non-string sequence suitable for slide expansion.

nodes.name: render.py
nodes.summary: Public format dispatcher that validates template/output combinations, routes DOCX, XLSX, and PPTX work to their renderers, and optionally converts results to PDF.

nodes.name: render
nodes.summary: Validates source and destination formats, invokes the appropriate Office renderer, and performs optional format-specific PDF conversion.

nodes.name: xlsx.py
nodes.summary: XLSX rendering engine that evaluates Jinja expressions in cells, expands row-loop blocks while preserving formatting, and translates copied formulas before serialization.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses openpyxl object copies and formula translation so expanded template rows retain workbook behavior and formatting.

nodes.name: render_xlsx
nodes.summary: Loads an XLSX workbook, renders every worksheet with strict Jinja semantics, and serializes the result with stage-aware errors.

nodes.name: _render_worksheet
nodes.summary: Expands worksheet loop blocks in reverse order before rendering every remaining cell value.

nodes.name: _find_loop_blocks
nodes.summary: Discovers and validates balanced row-level Jinja loops encoded in worksheet cells.

nodes.name: _single_text_cell_value
nodes.summary: Returns a stripped loop-marker string only when a cell contains an entire for or endfor tag.

nodes.name: _render_loop_block
nodes.summary: Replaces a loop-marker region with repeated, styled row templates rendered against per-item contexts.

nodes.name: _capture_row
nodes.summary: Creates shallow copies of a worksheet row for later loop expansion.

nodes.name: _copy_cell
nodes.summary: Copies styling, formatting, hyperlinks, and comments between openpyxl cells.

nodes.name: _render_cell_value
nodes.summary: Evaluates standalone Jinja expressions as typed values, renders inline templates, and translates formulas when rows are copied.

nodes.name: _evaluate_iterable
nodes.summary: Evaluates a Jinja row-loop expression and rejects strings or non-sequence results.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Defines the Stencil package's public API by re-exporting rendering, PDF-conversion, error, and version symbols.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Python's __all__ to make the supported package surface explicit.

nodes.name: _version.py
nodes.summary: Resolves the installed office-stencil package version from importlib metadata and provides a development fallback when metadata is unavailable.

nodes.name: pdf.py
nodes.summary: Provides a PDF conversion abstraction and a bounded LibreOffice subprocess implementation for rendered DOCX, XLSX, and PPTX bytes, including retries and detailed failure handling.
nodes.languageNotes: Combines a structural Protocol with a frozen dataclass so callers can inject alternate conversion workers while retaining an immutable default converter configuration.

nodes.name: ConversionWorker
nodes.summary: Defines the protocol implemented by workers that convert rendered Office document bytes into another byte format.

nodes.name: PdfConversionError
nodes.summary: Specializes RenderError for failures encountered while converting rendered Office bytes to PDF.

nodes.name: LibreOfficePdfConverter
nodes.summary: Converts Office bytes to PDF by running LibreOffice headlessly in isolated temporary workspaces, with executable discovery, timeouts, retries, and output validation.

nodes.name: convert_docx_to_pdf
nodes.summary: Converts rendered DOCX bytes to PDF using an injected conversion worker or the default LibreOffice converter.

nodes.name: convert_xlsx_to_pdf
nodes.summary: Converts rendered XLSX bytes to PDF using an injected conversion worker or the default LibreOffice converter.

nodes.name: convert_pptx_to_pdf
nodes.summary: Converts rendered PPTX bytes to PDF using an injected conversion worker or the default LibreOffice converter.

nodes.name: _summarize_process_output
nodes.summary: Combines, normalizes, and truncates LibreOffice stdout and stderr for concise conversion error messages.

nodes.name: publish.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions release pipeline that validates the Python package, builds distributions, derives release notes from the changelog, creates or updates a GitHub release, and publishes artifacts to PyPI on pushes to main or manual dispatch.
nodes.languageNotes: The workflow uses job-scoped write permissions, uv for reproducible Python setup and builds, and idempotent release commands that update existing GitHub releases and skip already-published PyPI files.

nodes.name: CHANGELOG.md
nodes.summary: Chronicles Stencil releases from 0.1.0 through 0.5.0, covering the DOCX foundation, PDF conversion, XLSX and PPTX engines, publishing safeguards, and the optional FastAPI service.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive project guide describing the public Python API, CLI, optional HTTP service, DOCX/XLSX/PPTX authoring rules, examples, development workflow, dependencies, publishing process, and architectural constraints.
nodes.languageNotes: The guide combines executable Python, shell, and TOML examples with format-specific authoring and troubleshooting guidance.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Defines the office-stencil Python 3.12 package, its runtime and optional dependencies, the stencil CLI entry point, Hatchling build settings, and uv, Ruff, pytest, and strict mypy configuration.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses PEP 621 project metadata with dependency groups and Hatchling's src-layout wheel configuration.

nodes.name: invoice.json
nodes.summary: Provides a complete sample invoice payload for DOCX rendering, including invoice metadata, customer details, repeatable line items, and a formatted total.

nodes.name: pptx-status.json
nodes.summary: Supplies project metadata and milestone records used to demonstrate placeholder replacement and repeated-slide generation in a PPTX status deck.

nodes.name: status-report.json
nodes.summary: Defines the sample project status-report payload used by the styled DOCX example, with nested project and owner values, milestone rows, and an optional risk note.

nodes.name: dependabot.yml
nodes.summary: Configures weekly Dependabot updates for the project's uv and GitHub Actions dependencies, grouping Python production and development packages.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Defines candidate exclusion patterns for Understand Anything codebase analysis, with grouped defaults and project-specific suggestions.

nodes.name: config.json
nodes.summary: Stores Understand Anything project preferences, currently selecting English for generated knowledge-graph content.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Explains the bundled DOCX and PPTX templates, their JSON payloads, authoring rules, and CLI and Python rendering workflows.

nodes.name: create_pptx_status_template.py
nodes.summary: Builds the styled PPTX status-deck fixture, including marker slides that demonstrate repeated-slide Jinja loops.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses python-pptx shape primitives and explicit OOXML-compatible slide geometry to create a deterministic template fixture.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Creates a presentation, assembles title, loop-marker, milestone, and next-step slides, then saves the template fixture.

nodes.name: _remove_default_slide
nodes.summary: Removes python-pptx's initial default slide before constructing the custom deck.

nodes.name: _blank_slide
nodes.summary: Adds a blank widescreen slide with the template's neutral background color.

nodes.name: _add_title_slide
nodes.summary: Composes the deck title slide with project placeholders, typography, and an accent rule.

nodes.name: _add_marker_slide
nodes.summary: Creates a marker slide containing a Jinja loop tag used by repeated-slide rendering.

nodes.name: _add_milestone_slide
nodes.summary: Creates the repeatable milestone detail slide with name, status, and summary placeholders.

nodes.name: _add_next_step_slide
nodes.summary: Builds a closing slide that prompts users to verify repeated milestone output.

nodes.name: _add_kicker
nodes.summary: Adds a small uppercase accent label to the top of a slide.

nodes.name: _add_rule
nodes.summary: Draws a thin teal accent rule at a specified slide position.

nodes.name: _add_status_pill
nodes.summary: Creates a styled rounded status badge whose text is supplied by a template placeholder.

nodes.name: _add_text
nodes.summary: Adds and styles a positioned text box using shared font, size, weight, and color options.

nodes.name: create_styled_status_template.py
nodes.summary: Generates the styled DOCX status-report fixture with headers, tables, section rules, placeholders, and conditional risk content.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses python-docx plus low-level OOXML elements for cell shading and paragraph borders that are not fully exposed by the high-level API.

nodes.name: shade_cell
nodes.summary: Applies an OOXML background-fill element to a Word table cell.

nodes.name: set_cell_text
nodes.summary: Sets a table cell's text in one editable run and applies optional bold and color styling.

nodes.name: add_section_heading
nodes.summary: Adds a blue section heading followed by a precisely styled OOXML bottom rule.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Assembles and saves the complete status-report template, including page chrome, health data, milestone table, and conditional risk note.

nodes.name: render_styled_status_pdf.py
nodes.summary: Demonstrates the Python API by loading status data, rendering the styled DOCX template to PDF, and writing the result.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Loads the example payload, invokes Stencil's render API for PDF output, and persists the generated bytes.

nodes.name: invoice.docx
nodes.summary: Provides the invoice DOCX fixture with Jinja variables, conditional notes, and a loop over line items.

nodes.name: pptx-status.pptx
nodes.summary: Provides the PPTX status-deck fixture with project placeholders and marker slides for cloning milestone slides.

nodes.name: styled-status-report.docx
nodes.summary: Provides a styled DOCX status-report fixture whose headers, tables, colors, and conditional content exercise formatting preservation.

nodes.name: python-package-cicd-agent-guide.md
nodes.summary: Detailed operational guide for Python package metadata, changelogs, pull-request CI, PyPI publishing, Dependabot, branch protection, releases, and failure recovery.

nodes.name: py.typed
nodes.summary: PEP 561 marker declaring that the distributed stencil package includes inline type information.
nodes.languageNotes: An empty py.typed marker is the standard PEP 561 signal for typed Python packages.

nodes.name: .gitkeep
nodes.summary: Keeps the tests directory tracked when no other test files are present in a minimal checkout.

nodes.name: test_api.py
nodes.summary: Tests the optional FastAPI adapter's rendering response, metrics accounting, template-root confinement, and render-error reporting.

nodes.name: _test_client
nodes.summary: Loads FastAPI's TestClient lazily and binds it to the application under test.

nodes.name: test_api_renders_document_and_records_metrics
nodes.summary: Verifies successful render responses, media headers, renderer arguments, and render-count metrics.

nodes.name: test_api_constrains_templates_to_configured_root
nodes.summary: Ensures API requests cannot escape the configured template root through parent-directory traversal.

nodes.name: test_api_records_render_failures
nodes.summary: Checks that render exceptions become client errors and increment stage-specific failure metrics.

nodes.name: test_render.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive integration suite for DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, and PDF rendering, including loops, style preservation, concurrency, and LibreOffice retry behavior.

nodes.name: _rendered_docx_text
nodes.summary: Extracts visible Word text from rendered DOCX package XML for output assertions.

nodes.name: _load_rendered_workbook
nodes.summary: Loads rendered XLSX bytes with formulas retained for workbook assertions.

nodes.name: _write_minimal_pptx
nodes.summary: Constructs a minimal valid PPTX ZIP package with deterministic slide XML and relationships for renderer tests.
nodes.languageNotes: Builds Open Packaging Convention parts directly so PPTX tests do not depend on presentation-authoring libraries.

nodes.name: _rendered_pptx_slide_texts
nodes.summary: Reads slide ordering and text from rendered PPTX relationships and slide XML.

nodes.name: test_render_rejects_unsupported_template_suffix
nodes.summary: Verifies that templates with unsupported file extensions are rejected.

nodes.name: test_render_rejects_unsupported_output_format
nodes.summary: Verifies that unsupported requested output formats are rejected.

nodes.name: test_xlsx_simple_substitutions_preserve_types_and_styles
nodes.summary: Checks XLSX substitutions preserve typed values, formulas, fonts, and workbook sheet structure.

nodes.name: test_xlsx_row_loop_clones_rows_and_translates_formulas
nodes.summary: Verifies worksheet row loops clone formatting and translate formulas for each generated row.

nodes.name: test_pptx_simple_substitutions_preserve_slide_order
nodes.summary: Checks PPTX text substitutions retain the source presentation's slide ordering.

nodes.name: test_pptx_slide_loop_clones_slides_and_rewrites_relationships
nodes.summary: Ensures slide-loop expansion clones slides and rewrites presentation relationships without duplicate identifiers.

nodes.name: test_render_converts_pptx_to_pdf
nodes.summary: Verifies PPTX-to-PDF dispatch passes rendered presentation bytes to the converter.

nodes.name: test_render_converts_xlsx_to_pdf
nodes.summary: Verifies XLSX-to-PDF dispatch passes rendered workbook bytes to the converter.

nodes.name: test_render_rejects_missing_docx_template
nodes.summary: Checks that rendering a missing DOCX template raises a stage-aware render error.

nodes.name: test_invoice_example_matches_expected_output_text
nodes.summary: Renders the invoice fixture and asserts expected data and conditional text appear in the DOCX output.

nodes.name: test_styled_status_report_example_matches_expected_output_text
nodes.summary: Renders the styled status fixture and validates substituted report text and conditional risk content.

nodes.name: test_pptx_status_example_matches_expected_output_text
nodes.summary: Renders the status deck and confirms project fields and repeated milestone slides contain expected text.

nodes.name: test_render_converts_docx_to_pdf
nodes.summary: Verifies DOCX-to-PDF dispatch invokes the configured converter with rendered document bytes.

nodes.name: test_render_pdf_dispatch_is_safe_for_concurrent_calls
nodes.summary: Exercises concurrent PDF renders to ensure per-call bytes remain isolated across worker threads.

nodes.name: test_libreoffice_converter_writes_pdf_and_cleans_temp_workspace
nodes.summary: Checks successful LibreOffice conversion returns PDF bytes and removes its temporary workspace.

nodes.name: test_libreoffice_converter_retries_transient_failure
nodes.summary: Verifies LibreOffice conversion retries a transient nonzero process result before succeeding.

nodes.name: test_libreoffice_converter_retries_after_timeout
nodes.summary: Verifies LibreOffice conversion retries after a subprocess timeout and cleans temporary state.

nodes.name: test_libreoffice_converter_reports_missing_executable
nodes.summary: Checks that a missing LibreOffice executable produces a clear PDF conversion error.

layers.name: Public Interfaces
layers.description: Package exports, the format-dispatch API, the Typer CLI, and the optional FastAPI adapter expose Stencil's rendering capabilities to callers.

layers.name: Rendering Engine
layers.description: Format-specific engines render DOCX, XLSX, and PPTX templates and convert rendered Office documents to PDF.

layers.name: Package Support
layers.description: Shared exception definitions, installed-version discovery, and PEP 561 typing metadata support the package's public and rendering modules.

layers.name: Examples and Fixtures
layers.description: Sample payloads, template fixtures, generator scripts, and walkthrough documentation demonstrate reproducible DOCX and PPTX rendering workflows.

layers.name: Test Layer
layers.description: Pytest integration coverage verifies the HTTP adapter and the full DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, and PDF rendering behavior.

layers.name: Project Configuration and Automation
layers.description: Packaging metadata, release automation, dependency updates, and Understand Anything settings govern how office-stencil is built, published, maintained, and analyzed.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: The project overview, release history, and Python package CI/CD guide explain usage, evolution, and maintenance practices.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand why Stencil exists, the Office formats it supports, and the architectural constraint of keeping format-specific XML work behind one stable rendering API. It also maps the Python, CLI, and optional HTTP entry points that the rest of this tour follows.

tour.title: Public Rendering Contract
tour.description: With the project goals established, inspect the package exports and central dispatcher that form the supported Python interface. The dispatcher validates template and output combinations, selects the DOCX, XLSX, or PPTX engine, and funnels failures through a shared exception hierarchy, so these files define the contract every adapter and engine must honor.
tour.languageLesson: Python packages often use __init__.py as a deliberate facade: re-exported symbols keep the public API stable even when implementation modules move. A py.typed marker tells type checkers that the installed package ships usable inline annotations under PEP 561.

tour.title: Command-Line Adapter
tour.description: The Typer CLI adapts the rendering contract from Step 2 for shell users by loading JSON data, invoking the same dispatcher, and translating domain failures into readable command errors. The package manifest connects that module to the installed stencil command while also defining dependencies, build metadata, and quality-tool configuration.
tour.languageLesson: A Python console-script entry point separates command discovery from implementation: the package manager installs an executable that imports and calls the configured Python object. This lets the CLI remain a thin adapter over reusable library code.

tour.title: DOCX Rendering
tour.description: The first format engine implements the dispatch contract by combining template validation with docxtpl-backed WordprocessingML rendering. The invoice and styled-report fixtures show why this layer matters: Jinja variables, conditions, repeated rows, and existing document formatting must survive as the engine produces serialized DOCX bytes.
tour.languageLesson: DOCX files are ZIP packages containing related XML parts. Template engines must preserve WordprocessingML structure and styles while replacing content, because visible text may be split across multiple XML runs.

tour.title: XLSX Rendering
tour.description: Building on the same dispatcher boundary, the spreadsheet engine evaluates Jinja expressions in cells and expands loop blocks into new rows. Its distinguishing responsibility is preserving styles while translating copied formulas, which keeps generated workbooks both visually consistent and computationally correct.
tour.languageLesson: Spreadsheet formulas use relative and absolute references, so copying a rendered row requires translating formulas rather than duplicating their text verbatim. XLSX is also an XML package, but cell styles and formulas live in coordinated workbook parts.

tour.title: PPTX Rendering
tour.description: The presentation engine applies the shared rendering model to a more structural task: editing Open Packaging Convention parts, rendering text across XML runs, and cloning marker slides for repeated data. The status-deck fixture and its JSON payload make the relationship concrete by pairing project placeholders with repeatable milestone slides.
tour.languageLesson: Cloning a PPTX slide requires more than copying its XML: presentation ordering, relationship IDs, and content-type declarations must remain consistent across the package. Run-aware text rendering is necessary because PowerPoint may split one visible placeholder across several XML elements.

tour.title: PDF Conversion Boundary
tour.description: After any Office engine returns document bytes, the dispatcher can hand them to the PDF abstraction. The LibreOffice-backed implementation isolates subprocess execution, time bounds, retries, and detailed failure reporting, while the example script demonstrates the complete Python flow from JSON data through DOCX rendering to a final PDF.
tour.languageLesson: A subprocess boundary should treat timeouts, exit status, temporary files, and retries as explicit failure modes. Wrapping those details behind an abstraction keeps the rendering API deterministic even though conversion depends on an external office suite.

tour.title: Optional HTTP Service
tour.description: The FastAPI module exposes the Step 2 contract to trusted internal callers without moving rendering logic into the web layer. It adds health and metrics endpoints, confines template lookup to an allowed root, and records in-process telemetry; its dedicated tests verify both successful responses and the security and error boundaries.
tour.languageLesson: FastAPI adapters benefit from keeping validation and transport concerns at the boundary while delegating domain work to ordinary Python functions. Resolving a requested path against an approved root is an important defense against directory traversal.

tour.title: Executable Examples
tour.description: The examples guide turns the engine concepts from Steps 4-7 into reproducible authoring and rendering workflows. Sample JSON payloads and template-generator scripts reveal the exact data shapes and marker conventions expected by DOCX and PPTX templates, making this the best place to experiment before creating production templates.

tour.title: Integration Verification
tour.description: Once the examples establish expected behavior, the main integration suite shows how the project protects it across DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, and PDF. Read these tests as an executable specification for loops, formatting preservation, concurrency, and LibreOffice retry behavior, especially when changing a format engine or the dispatcher.
tour.languageLesson: Pytest fixtures and parameterized cases are useful for expressing one behavioral contract across multiple formats. Integration tests at package boundaries catch coordination failures that isolated unit tests of XML helpers or subprocess wrappers may miss.

tour.title: Release and Maintenance
tour.description: Finish with the automation that turns verified code into a published package. The workflow validates and builds distributions, derives release notes from the changelog, manages the GitHub release, and publishes to PyPI, while Dependabot and the operational guide document dependency upkeep and recovery procedures.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions workflows use event triggers, jobs, and ordered steps to encode release gates. Keeping package validation, artifact building, release creation, and publishing as distinct stages makes failures easier to diagnose and reduces the risk of publishing an unverified distribution.
